name: Imunon, Inc.
business: Imunon is a clinical-stage biotechnology company focused on advancing a portfolio of innovative treatments that harness the body's natural mechanisms, developing non-viral DNA technology across its TheraPlas and PlaCCine modalities. Its lead program, IMNN-001, is a DNA-based immunotherapy for the localized treatment of advanced ovarian cancer that has completed Phase II and is currently conducting a Phase 3 clinical trial. The company also has a DNA-based COVID-19 booster vaccine candidate, IMNN-101, which has completed a first-in-human study.

exposures.sentence: Imunon is a clinical-stage biotechnology company focused on advancing a portfolio of innovative treatments that harness the body’s natural mechanisms with the aim to generate safe, effective and durable responses across a broad array of human diseases, constituting a differentiating approach from conventional therapies.
The second modality, PlaCCine, is developed for the coding of viral antigens that can elicit a strong immunological response.

catalysts.event: Positive topline results from Phase 2 OVATION 2 Study announced
catalysts.sentence: On July 30, 2024, the Company announced positive topline results from the Phase I/II OVATION 2 Study.

catalysts.event: End-of-Phase 2 meeting with FDA to discuss Phase 3 trial design
catalysts.sentence: The EOP2 meeting was conducted in the fourth quarter of 2024.

catalysts.event: Phase 3 OVATION 3 trial initiation and enrollment ahead of forecast
catalysts.sentence: In March 2025, the Company announced that the FDA is aligned with the protocol for the Phase 3 OVATION 3 pivotal trial of its lead candidate IMNN-001 in development for the treatment of women with newly diagnosed advanced ovarian cancer.

catalysts.event: Final data from Phase 2 OVATION 2 Study announced, showing median 14.7 month OS increase
catalysts.sentence: On March 25, 2026, the Company announced final data from the completed Phase 2 OVATION 2 clinical trial evaluating IMNN-001 in combination with standard of care (SoC) neoadjuvant and adjuvant chemotherapy.
